topic: Sharp non-equivalence theorem for local-projection (LP) versus structural-vector-autoregression (SVAR) identification of structural impulse responses under non-Gaussian innovations and partial sign restrictions.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the sharp boundary at which LP restrictions and non-Gaussian/sign-restricted SVAR restrictions identify the same structural impulse-response set, and when they identify distinct functionals.

source_refs.claim: LPs and VARs estimate the same impulse responses under unrestricted lag structures, and VAR identification schemes including sign restrictions can equivalently be implemented with LPs.
source_refs.author: Mikkel Plagborg-Moller and Christian K. Wolf

source_refs.claim: Non-Gaussian independent structural shocks identify the SVAR impact matrix up to scaling and permutation, with economic labeling supplied by outside restrictions.
source_refs.author: Markku Lanne, Mika Meitz, and Pentti Saikkonen

source_refs.claim: Independence restrictions can refine sign-restricted VAR identified sets, but the resulting sets may be local, non-convex, and sensitive to weak higher-moment information.
source_refs.author: Thorsten Drautzburg and Jonathan H. Wright

source_refs.excerpt: the limiting regular identified content is determined by a thresholded singular subspace
endpoint value in Theta_cell that violates the shared-completion constraints

open_problems.why_unsolved: The LP/SVAR equivalence literature treats a common reduced-form impulse-response estimand under linear lag representations, while non-Gaussian SVAR identification uses higher moments of a shared innovation system and still leaves scale, sign, permutation, and economic-label ambiguity. The prior catalogue contains adjacent row-space and shared-completion gaps, but not the time-series theorem that separates LP projection rows from one shared ICA/sign-restricted structural rotation. The concrete obstacle is reconciling horizonwise LP restrictions with a single cross-equation, cross-horizon structural completion.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient algebraic characterization of the common LP/SVAR identified IRF set, plus an explicit finite-dimensional counterexample where the reduced-form projection objects agree but the non-Gaussian/sign-restricted SVAR functional differs.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine when horizonwise LP moment restrictions can be pasted into one admissible non-Gaussian SVAR shock law, and when the common-shock law creates strict non-equivalence.

source_refs.claim: Structural identification can be made portable between LPs and VARs when both target the same population impulse-response object.
source_refs.author: Mikkel Plagborg-Moller and Christian K. Wolf

source_refs.claim: Independent-component methods remove the usual SVAR rotation non-identifiability when structural shocks are independent and at most one component is Gaussian.
source_refs.author: Christian Gourieroux, Alain Monfort, and Jean-Paul Renne

source_refs.claim: Across many macro DGPs, LP and VAR estimators occupy different bias-variance positions, and conclusions vary with horizon and identification design.
source_refs.author: Dake Li, Mikkel Plagborg-Moller, and Christian K. Wolf

source_refs.excerpt: Include an explicit single-X, binary-outcome ... LP witness showing strict endpoint improvement
state the pasting conjecture as a measurable common-latent construction

open_problems.why_unsolved: ICA-based SVAR identification is residual-system based: it asks for one innovation vector and one mixing matrix whose components satisfy higher-moment independence restrictions. LPs instead deliver horizon-by-horizon projection moments, and the catalogue repeatedly flags that separate marginal or coarsened LP witnesses need not paste into a single latent law. The unresolved issue is whether LP moments admit a direct non-Gaussian structural completion, or only an outer relaxation of the SVAR feasible set.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A compatibility theorem for stacked LP moments, non-Gaussian innovation distributions, mixing matrices, and partial sign restrictions, identifying exactly when one common SVAR law realizes all LP-implied responses and giving a rational witness when it cannot.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove or refute strict non-rectangularity of the joint LP/SVAR identified set when projection restrictions and non-Gaussian sign restrictions are optimized together rather than separately.

source_refs.excerpt: strictly below the rectangular diagnostic envelope
standard Balke-Pearl finite latent + rectangular no-go

open_problems.why_unsolved: A prior downgraded proposal identified rectangular diagnostic envelopes as too generic unless tied to a named published target and a joint-program proof. The LP/SVAR topic supplies the named target, but still lacks a theorem showing whether the product of an LP projection envelope and an SVAR higher-moment/sign envelope is strictly larger than the sharp joint feasible set. The obstacle is that higher cumulants, sign inequalities, and projection equations couple the same latent shock law rather than independent nuisance components.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp-set theorem defining the joint feasible LP/SVAR program and its rectangular relaxation, proving equality or strict containment on an open parameter region with a support-function or Farkas certificate.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop robust inference for non-Gaussian or sign-refined SVAR impulse responses under finite-lag misspecification, matching the recent double-robust LP theory or proving that no analogous SVAR result is possible.

source_refs.claim: Conventional LP confidence intervals retain coverage under local VAR misspecification, whereas short-to-moderate lag VAR intervals can severely undercover.
source_refs.author: Jose Luis Montiel Olea, Mikkel Plagborg-Moller, Eric Qian, and Christian K. Wolf

source_refs.claim: Lag-augmented LP inference with normal critical values is uniformly valid over stationary and nonstationary data and a wide range of response horizons.
source_refs.author: Jose Luis Montiel Olea and Mikkel Plagborg-Moller

source_refs.claim: Non-Gaussian SVAR identification and asymptotic inference are developed after estimating a finite-order VAR model with independent non-Gaussian structural innovations.
source_refs.author: Markku Lanne, Mika Meitz, and Pentti Saikkonen

open_problems.why_unsolved: The new LP robustness results concern reduced-form dynamic misspecification and projection inference, while non-Gaussian SVAR identification depends on higher moments of estimated innovations. Lag truncation can distort both second-moment dynamics and the higher-cumulant deconvolution used for structural labeling, and existing non-Gaussian SVAR inference does not characterize coverage in that local-misspecification regime.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A local-misspecification asymptotic theorem for ICA/sign-refined SVAR IRFs, giving uniformly valid confidence sets or an impossibility result that forces long-lag VARs, lag-augmented LP moments, or explicit robustness corrections.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct frequentist-valid confidence sets for impulse-response identified sets combining weak external instruments, partial sign/zero restrictions, and weak non-Gaussian independence restrictions.

source_refs.claim: External-instrument SVAR inference is closely analogous to weak-IV inference, motivating Fieller and Anderson-Rubin style confidence sets for impulse responses.
source_refs.author: Jose Luis Montiel Olea, James H. Stock, and Mark W. Watson

source_refs.claim: Bayesian proxy-SVAR algorithms can combine proxies with zero and sign restrictions to jointly identify structural shocks.
source_refs.author: Jonas E. Arias, Juan F. Rubio-Ramirez, and Daniel F. Waggoner

source_refs.claim: Higher-moment independence restrictions can refine sign-restricted identified sets while remaining robust to weak or absent higher-moment identification.
source_refs.author: Thorsten Drautzburg and Jonathan H. Wright

open_problems.why_unsolved: Separate methods handle weak proxy instruments, Bayesian proxy-SVARs with sign/zero restrictions, and independence-based refinements of sign-restricted sets. A unified frequentist theory must remain valid when the proxy is weak, the non-Gaussian cumulant gap is near zero, and the admissible rotations form a non-convex partially identified set. No cited paper supplies a single test-inversion object spanning all three weak-identification margins.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A projection or test-inversion confidence-set construction with uniform coverage over weak-IV, near-Gaussian, and set-identified sign/zero regimes, allowing intersections of proxy moments, sign inequalities, zero restrictions, and independence moments.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize identification of sign-restricted structural impulse responses when shocks are non-Gaussian and orthogonal but not mutually independent.

source_refs.claim: The independence assumption in much non-Gaussian SVAR identification is stronger than the standard SVAR orthogonality assumption; skewness or excess kurtosis can identify some shocks without mutual independence.
source_refs.author: Markku Lanne and Keyan Liu

source_refs.claim: GMM approaches to non-Gaussian SVARs can deliver local identification from suitable higher-moment conditions, with identification tied to rank conditions.
source_refs.author: Alain Guay

source_refs.claim: Refining sign restrictions through independence naturally requires true structural shocks to be independent, not merely uncorrelated.
source_refs.author: Thorsten Drautzburg and Jonathan H. Wright

open_problems.why_unsolved: Independence-based SVAR methods can fail if true shocks are only orthogonal, while newer orthogonality-plus-higher-moment identification gives partial or rank-condition-dependent recovery. The interaction with partial sign/zero restrictions and LP/SVAR equivalence is not characterized: sign restrictions may label shocks that higher moments only partially separate, and LP projection objects do not expose the same dependence structure.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identified-set geometry for orthogonal-but-dependent non-Gaussian shocks under sign/zero restrictions, including necessary rank or cumulant conditions and a specialization that collapses to the independent ICA case.

literature_map:
Jorda (2005) introduced LP impulse-response estimation as a robust alternative to VAR extrapolation.
Plagborg-Moller and Wolf (2021, Econometrica) show LPs and VARs estimate the same IRFs under unrestricted lag structures and make identification schemes portable.
Montiel Olea and Plagborg-Moller (2021, Econometrica) establish robust LP inference for persistence and long horizons.
Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, Qian, and Wolf (2024, Econometrica forthcoming) create a new tension: LP intervals are double-robust to lag misspecification while finite-lag VAR intervals can undercover.
Li, Plagborg-Moller, and Wolf (2024, Journal of Econometrics) document a bias-variance frontier where LP and VAR conclusions vary by horizon and identification design.
Stock and Watson (2018, Economic Journal) and Montiel Olea, Stock, and Watson (2021, Journal of Econometrics) give the external-instrument/proxy-SVAR bridge and weak-IV inference issues.
Arias, Rubio-Ramirez, and Waggoner (2021, Journal of Econometrics) combine proxy-SVARs with sign and zero restrictions.
Baumeister and Hamilton (2015, Econometrica) show sign-restricted SVAR conclusions can be prior-driven in underidentified settings.
Read (2022, Econometrics Journal) and related sign/zero restriction work sharpen algorithms and exact restrictions for set-identified SVARs.
Gourieroux, Monfort, and Renne (2017) and Lanne, Meitz, and Saikkonen (2017, Journal of Econometrics) use non-Gaussian independence/ICA to identify SVAR shocks up to scale and permutation.
Guay (2021, JBES) supplies GMM identification for non-Gaussian SVARs and highlights local rank-condition identification.
Drautzburg and Wright (2023, Journal of Econometrics) refine sign-restricted sets using independence, explicitly allowing weak or absent higher-moment identification and non-convex IRF sets.
Lanne and Liu (2026, Econometrics Journal) relax independence toward potentially dependent non-Gaussian shocks, making orthogonality versus independence a live frontier.
The unresolved methodological knot is how LP/SVAR equivalence, lag misspecification, non-Gaussian statistical identification, weak proxies, and partial sign restrictions jointly determine sharp structural IRF identified sets.
prior_proposal_map:
Target qid eid_lp_svar_nonequiv/v1 currently has only state and init pipeline files; no target proposal or reviews were present.
Closest reusable proposal family is q1_spectral_phase_transition: row-space/spectral point-partial-nonidentification frontiers and minimal-basis change.
Closest LP/polytope family is pid_dynamic_iv_compliance: full-history feasible set versus coarsened/Markovized projection gaps, with reviewer-prescribed explicit LP witnesses.
Closest shared-compatibility family is pid_network_exposure: product/cellwise relaxation versus one shared hidden completion, including a reviewer strengthening path for shared-completion violations.
Closest common-latent-law family is pid_dose_response_iv: rank/copula/common-law pasting and sign-certification witnesses.
Closest non-rectangularity family is pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec: joint latent-response program versus rectangular diagnostic envelope, downgraded for weak published target.
Useful finite-linear template is q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends_v2: target-loaded null directions make compressed reported summaries nonsharp.
No mined prior source mentions Plagborg-Moller/Wolf, SVAR, structural impulse responses, or non-Gaussian innovations directly; prior-proposal entries are adjacent algebraic templates cross-linked only when the mathematical gap matches the web literature.
